Title: Improving cassava bacterial blight resistance by editing the epigenome

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports pathogen-induced sugar efflux and contributes to cassava bacterial blight symptom development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induction of MeSWEET10a promotes water-soaking symptoms and full virulence; blocking its induction reduces cassava bacterial blight symptoms
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeSWEET10a is the host susceptibility gene targeted by Xam TAL20. 2. Targeted promoter methylation at the TAL20 effector binding element blocks TAL20-mediated transcriptional activation of MeSWEET10a in vivo. 3. Reduced MeSWEET10a induction is associated with decreased water-soaking lesion area and intensity without obvious growth defects.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR after Xam infection; targeted promoter methylation via DMS3-ZF; EMSA showing methylation inhibits TAL20 binding; disease symptom quantification
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced during Xanthomonas infection in wild type; suppressed in methylated promoter lines
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced during Xanthomonas infection in wild type
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Xanthomonas phaseoli pv. manihotis infection; TAL20 presence or deletion mutant comparison
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No gross effect on root and shoot growth and development reported under greenhouse and field conditions
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Targeted de novo CG methylation at the promoter effector binding element using DMS3-ZF prevents TAL20 binding and represses pathogen-induced expression
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native MeSWEET10a promoter targeted at TAL20 EBE
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene is a susceptibility gene rather than a nutritional biofortification target; included because it is the central experimentally manipulated host gene. The study is disease resistance focused, not nutrient metabolite engineering.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing or epigenetically modifying the MeSWEET10a promoter EBE is a promising strategy to improve cassava bacterial blight resistance while preserving normal growth

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: RNA-directed DNA methylation factor; DMS3 family
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirectly limits pathogen exploitation of host sugar transport by preventing induction of MeSWEET10a
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: When fused to a synthetic zinc finger and targeted to the MeSWEET10a promoter, DMS3 establishes de novo methylation that blocks TAL20-dependent activation and reduces cassava bacterial blight symptoms
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis DMS3 fused to a synthetic zinc finger targeted the TAL20 effector binding element in the cassava MeSWEET10a promoter. 2. DMS3-ZF caused specific de novo methylation, mainly CG methylation, at the target locus. 3. This methylation blocked pathogen effector-mediated MeSWEET10a induction and reduced water-soaking disease symptoms without obvious growth penalties.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression of DMS3-ZF; amplicon bisulfite sequencing; whole-genome bisulfite sequencing; RT-qPCR; disease lesion quantification
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MeSWEET10a promoter TAL20 effector binding element
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses pathogen-induced transcriptional activation of MeSWEET10a by installing promoter methylation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Epigenetic repression of pathogen-induced sugar transporter expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Targeted DNA methylation / chromatin-based transcriptional repression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Synthetic zinc-finger targeting module; pathogen TAL20 challenge used to reveal regulatory effect
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions as a component of the RNA-directed DNA methylation pathway; used here as DMS3-ZF fusion
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically expressed in cassava DMS3-ZF lines
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Xanthomonas phaseoli pv. manihotis infection; greenhouse and field growth evaluation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No gross defects in root and shoot growth and development; off-target methylation appeared limited
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Acts as the effector that deposits targeted methylation at the MeSWEET10a promoter in the transgenic system
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key engineered epigenetic effector enabling methylation-based silencing of the susceptibility gene response
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The focal phenotype is disease resistance rather than improved nutrient content; DMS3 is not native cassava and was deployed as a synthetic fusion protein
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports epigenome editing strategies for crop improvement, especially resistance traits via promoter methylation without altering DNA sequence
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: DMS3 genomic coding sequence including promoter cloned into pEG302 backbone
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Amplicon bisulfite sequencing; whole-genome bisulfite sequencing (GEO: GSE187022; GSM5667182, GSM5667183, GSM5667184)
